It’s  important to involve your child in the process, not just take over yourself, because the main goal is to help him or her  strengthen the self-confidence that might’ve been shaken and restore a sense of physical and/or emotional safety.    5. What if my child witnesses cyberbullying or bullies others?    If your child does experience cyberbullying as a witness or bystander, it’s important to talk through some strategies as to how  they can help their peers. Being kind goes a long way.
